payload.claim_text: After our first year of the program, we were still manually uploading every single open dataset to the open data portal. We desperately needed the expertise of a data engineer to scale the program.
payload.description: First year of program used manual uploads; data engineer expertise was needed for scaling

records.raw_text: After our first year of the program, we were still manually uploading every single open dataset to the open data portal. We desperately needed the expertise of a data engineer to scale the program in a sustainable way.

payload.claim_text: A team inbox (opendata@mtahq.org) has worked well for us.
payload.description: Open data program team email contact

records.raw_text: A team inbox (opendata@mtahq.org) has worked well for us.

payload.claim_text: it's now an award-winning program that can serve as a model for other government agencies
payload.description: MTA open data program is award-winning and serves as a model for other agencies

records.raw_text: it's now an award-winning program that can serve as a model for other government agencies standing up their own open data programs.

payload.claim_text: We put quite a bit of work into building our data visualization tool metrics.mta.info early on, which visualizes open datasets in a way that is easily digestible for people of all technical skill levels.
payload.description: MTA built metrics.mta.info as a data visualization tool for open data

records.raw_text: We put quite a bit of work into building our data visualization tool metrics.mta.info early on

records.display_name: First US transit agency to publish operating budget as open data

payload.claim_text: the operating budget (the first transit agency in the United States to do so!)
payload.description: MTA was the first transit agency in the United States to publish its operating budget as open data

records.raw_text: the operating budget (the first transit agency in the United States to do so!)

payload.claim_text: When identifying and preparing first datasets for release, prioritize easy wins: data already made public in some form or data frequently FOILed.
payload.description: Strategy to focus on data already public (e.g. PDFs) or frequently FOILed to avoid needing formalized data governance policy first

records.raw_text: When identifying and preparing your first datasets for release, we recommend prioritizing easy wins, such as data that is already made public (but not open) in some form or data that is frequently requested through the FOIL process.

records.display_name: MTA Open Data Law key provisions

payload.claim_text: The MTA's open data law requires: Designate a data coordinator; Create a three-year plan to release all publishable data; Define a portal to contribute to (data.ny.gov).
payload.description: Key provisions that helped establish the MTA Open Data Program

records.raw_text: A few pieces of the legislation were particularly helpful in establishing how the program would work: Designate a data coordinator, who is responsible for managing the program and complying with the law. Create a three-year plan to release all publishable data. Define a portal to contribute to, which in our case, was New York State's open data portal at data.ny.gov.

records.display_name: Real-time vehicle arrival data published since 2012

payload.claim_text: real-time vehicle arrival data feeds published in open format since 2012
payload.description: First MTA open data available before the Open Data Law; real-time vehicle arrival data feeds have been published in open format since 2012

records.raw_text: real-time vehicle arrival data feeds, which we have published in open format since 2012

records.display_name: MTA publishes annual open data program update

payload.claim_text: While not included in our statutory requirements, we still publish an annual update on our program page, which we have found incredibly valuable in memorializing our success.
payload.description: MTA publishes annual open data program updates voluntarily

records.raw_text: While not included in our statutory requirements, we still publish an annual update on our program page

records.display_name: Open Data Program key player roles

payload.claim_text: Key players for starting an open data program: Program manager (day-to-day operations, liaison), Data scientists (write code to produce datasets), Data engineers (architect automation tooling), Internal leadership (executive sponsorship), Legal and Communications leadership, Internal users (cross-departmental data sharing), External users and advocates.
payload.description: Seven key contributor roles identified for a successful open data program

records.raw_text: Key players Identifying who needs to be part of the immediate team... Program manager... Data scientist(s)... Data engineer(s)... Internal leadership... Legal serves as an important safeguard... Internal users... External users and advocates...
Lisa Mae Fiedler manages MTA's Open Data program.
